title: はじめまして
en: Pleased to meet you. My name is Tanaka. I'm Japanese. I study Japanese at a university in Tokyo. I talk with my teacher every day.

title: いい天気
en: The weather is nice today. The sky is blue. You can see the mountains clearly. The children are watching the water in the river.

title: あいさつ 2
en: I am a university student. I'm a freshman. Are you a high school student? Are you alive?

title: あいさつ 3
en: Life is good. Are you a student? What's your younger brother's name? Is this your real name?

title: 学校のこと
en: I am a university student. How's school? I'm going to school. That's a school.

title: 学校のこと 2
en: Are you a student? Are you a high school student? There comes our teacher. It was last week.

title: 学校のこと 3
en: They're only words. Money up front. I'm a freshman. Are you alive?

title: 食べ物の話
en: Dinner's ready. I've eaten. What did you eat? I already ate.

title: 食べ物の話 3
en: Eat something. I have to eat. Why don't we eat? You need to eat.

title: 今日の天気
en: What horrible weather! Is the weather nice? Do you mean what you say? Care killed a cat.

title: 時間
en: What time do you have? It is seven now. It's already seven. It's six o'clock already.

title: 時間 2
en: See you a little before 7. It is close to seven o'clock. Two o'clock would be OK. Would two o'clock be all right?

title: 時間 3
en: It's 3:30. There's no time. Time is money. It's 2:30 now.

title: 数字 2
en: One more day to go. I'm traveling alone. Let me have a cigarette. Go there by yourself.

title: 旅行 2
en: Let's make a phone call. Call me. I'll phone you. Here comes the train.

title: 旅行 3
en: Turn on the lights. Let's go by car. Stop the car. Do you need the car?

title: 料理 2
en: I ate too much. I've eaten. What did you eat? I already ate.

title: 乗り物 2
en: Do you want a car? Do you need the car? I'm in the car now. It was that car.

title: 友達と 2
en: I'm talking on the phone. Let's make a phone call. The number is engaged. The line is busy.

title: 友達と 3
en: Call me. Please continue. I'll phone you. It's hard to speak.

title: 技術
en: I'm talking on the phone. Let's make a phone call. Oh, the lights went out. Call me.

title: 技術 2
en: I'll phone you. Put out the light. Here comes the train. Turn on the lights.

title: 技術 3
en: The story wandered. The number is engaged. The line is busy. Please continue.

tokens.t: ょんきりのちょんさんのほんとうの

title: 私の一日
en: Every day I go to school. At school I learn Japanese and English. At lunch I eat bread. In the evening I read a book at home.

title: 朝の生活
en: I wake up at six every morning. I eat bread for breakfast. Then I drink coffee. At eight, I go to school.

title: 私の家族
en: There are four people in my family. My father is a company employee, and my mother is a teacher. My older brother is studying at a university in Tokyo. I love to play with the cat at home.

title: レストランで
en: I went to a restaurant with my friend. I ate fish, and my friend ate meat. It was very delicious. I'd like to come back again next week.

title: 学校の一日
en: I'm a middle-school student. The school is near my house. I walk there every day. The maths teacher is very kind.

title: 週末
en: I don't go to school on Saturdays and Sundays. I get up slowly in the morning. I have coffee with friends at a café. In the evening I watch a film at home.

title: 買い物
en: Today I went shopping with my mother. We bought vegetables and meat at a nearby supermarket. Then we got bread at the bakery. After getting home, we made dinner.

title: 夏休みの思い出
en: Last summer I went to the sea with my family. Every day we swam and collected seashells. At night we watched fireworks on the beach. It was a very fun week.

title: あいさつ
en: There comes our teacher. It was last week. They're only words. Money up front.

title: 家族のこと
en: My father is coming. I have an older brother. I don't have an older sister. What's your younger brother's name?

title: 家族のこと 2
en: I leave to go home. The house is on fire! Come home. Come to my house.

title: 家族のこと 3
en: I see a house. Are you going home? Do you have a big family? Were you home?

title: 食べ物の話 2
en: What do you want to eat? Eat! I eat fish. A fish eats.

title: 今日の天気 2
en: Are you losing your mind? He came to himself. Cheer up! You seem to be a little under the weather.

title: 今日の天気 3
en: How are you doing? You like it, huh? Never mind! Yo, my man!

title: 数字
en: He who steals a pin will steal an ox. How about a bite to eat? I'm alone. I'm a freshman.

title: 数字 3
en: Let's have a cigarette. I'll go alone. It's 2:30 now. Scant difference.

title: 体
en: Handsome is that handsome does. Open your eyes. Are your eyes bad? Are you awake?

title: 体 2
en: You keep out of this. You're all talk and no action! How about a bite to eat? They're only words.

title: 体 3
en: Put your hands down! Put your hands up! I'm at a complete loss! Show me your hands.

title: 家のこと
en: I leave to go home. The house is on fire! Come home. Come to my house.

title: 家のこと 2
en: I see a house. Are you going home? Do you have a big family? Were you home?

title: 家のこと 3
en: Come to my house. I'll do it at home. I bought a house. It's a big house.

title: 旅行
en: Enjoy your trip. I'm traveling alone. I'm at Tokyo Station now. I'm talking on the phone.

title: 買い物
en: What did you buy? I'll buy it. It's a good time to buy. I bought a house.

title: 買い物 2
en: Buy me the book. I bought flowers. Why don't you buy a car? Do you have everything?

title: 趣味
en: It leaves a bad aftertaste. The fish doesn't taste good. Today is our regular closing day. You can't be so sure.

title: 音楽
en: Are you fond of music? What was that sound? What is this sound? I hear footsteps.

title: 音楽 3
en: Enjoy your trip. It's comfortable. I enjoyed myself. Are you enjoying it?

title: 料理
en: Are you a good cook? What did you make? Can you make it? Dinner's ready.

title: 料理 3
en: What do you want to eat? I'm fasting. Eat! I eat fish.

title: 乗り物
en: Get into the car. Let's go by car. Stop the car. Here comes the train.

title: 仕事
en: Can you meet tonight? Come and see me. I'm an office worker. See you later!

title: 仕事 2
en: Can we meet tomorrow? I miss you! Let's get down to business. I am working.

title: 仕事 3
en: How's work? Get back to work! Are you at work? Are you still at work?

title: 気持ち 2
en: Enjoy your trip. Are you fond of music? It's comfortable. I enjoyed myself.

title: 季節 3
en: I'm Natsuko. The days are long in the summer. The summer is over. I like summer best.

title: 自然 2
en: I like wild flowers. Did you see the fireworks? I bought flowers. Don't break the branches.

title: 町と村
en: Can you meet tonight? Come and see me. I'm an office worker. See you later!

title: 体の調子 3
en: Cheer up! You seem to be a little under the weather. How are you doing? How goes it?

title: 勉強 2
en: I'm going to school. That's a school. Are you a student? Don't study.

title: 勉強 3
en: He comes on strong. Have the devil's own luck. Tom is the strongest. It's windy.

title: 一寸法師
author: 楠山 正雄
en: Long, long ago, in a certain place, there lived an old man and an old woman. They had no child, and were very lonely about it. So one day they prayed to the local shrine, asking: please grant us a child — even one no bigger than the tip of a finger.

title: 大工と鬼六
author: 楠山 正雄
en: Long, long ago, in a certain place, there flowed a great river. It was so swift and turbulent that even the most skilled carpenter could never build a bridge across it. Every time one was built, the very next day a flood would come and wash it away.

title: ねずみの嫁入り
author: 楠山 正雄
en: Long ago, in a certain country, there was a mouse father and mother who had one beautiful daughter. They wanted to give her in marriage to the very greatest being in all the world. After much thought, they decided that nothing in the world was greater than the sun — so they would marry her to the sun.

tokens.r: おおくにぬしのみこと
あまのひぼこのみこと

tokens.t: ょうしはいつものように

title: 今日の天気
en: Today the weather is very nice. The sky is blue and the clouds are white. Let's take a walk in the park. The dog will come along too.

title: うちの犬
en: We have a small dog at home. Her name is Pochi. Every morning we walk together in the park. Pochi loves playing with a ball.

title: 私の趣味
en: My hobby is reading. I often go to the library on weekends. I like reading books in a quiet place. Recently I've been reading Japanese novels.

title: 東京へ
en: Tomorrow I'm going to Tokyo on the shinkansen. My friend is waiting at Tokyo Station. We plan to visit an art museum together. In the evening we'll have dinner together.

title: 買い物 3
en: He is a big shot. It's 50 yen. No merchandise can be returned. It’s nice quality.

title: 趣味 2
en: It's the truth. Do you? Why? Do you mean what you say? Don't open your book.

title: 趣味 3
en: I'd like a book. Oh no, really? It's true. It's this book.

title: スポーツ
en: He ran. He ran. Keep running. I kept running.

title: スポーツ 2
en: He can swim. Can you swim? Let's swim. I'd like to swim.

title: スポーツ 3
en: Don't you swim? Are you able to swim well? It's a godsend. Have the devil's own luck.

title: 音楽 2
en: Let's live it up! No pain, no gains. Have fun. Make yourself at home.

title: 乗り物 3
en: Why don't you buy a car? The car is fast. I stole a car. I'm talking on the phone.

title: 友達と
en: Are you friends? Are you friends? Is he a friend? The story wandered.

title: 動物
en: This is a dog. Are you afraid of dogs? It's a big dog. Dogs are funny.

title: 動物 2
en: It's just a dog. That's my cat. Do you like black cats? I am a cat.

title: 動物 3
en: The cat isn't sleeping. I like catgirls. I eat fish. A fish eats.

title: 気持ち
en: Let's live it up! No pain, no gains. Have fun. Make yourself at home.

title: 気持ち 3
en: Are you enjoying it? Was it fun? I really like him! I love you.

title: 季節
en: Spring is here. The summer is over. I feel more tired in the wintertime. Winter has gone.

title: 季節 2
en: Winter is cold. I want to hibernate. It'll soon be spring. Summer is gone.

title: 町と村 2
en: Can we meet tomorrow? I miss you! Do you have everything? He is a big shot.

title: 町と村 3
en: It's a cargo ship. It's an industrial city. See you tomorrow in the office. Let's meet on Sunday.

title: 体の調子
en: Care killed a cat. I get sick a lot. Do you mean what you say? He is quick of temper.

title: 体の調子 2
en: He comes on strong. Haste is waste. Are you losing your mind? He came to himself.

title: 勉強
en: He acquired education late in his life. I am a university student. How's school? Do you like school?

title: 桃太郎
author: 楠山 正雄
en: Long, long ago, there lived an old man and an old woman. Every day the old man went to the mountain to gather firewood, and the old woman went to the river to do the washing. One day, while the old woman was busy washing by the river, a huge peach came floating down from upstream — bobbing along.

title: 金太郎
author: 楠山 正雄
en: Long, long ago, deep in the mountains of Ashigara, there lived a little boy called Kintarō. His mother, Yamauba, was a mountain witch, and so the boy was very strong from the day he was born — strong enough to wrestle bears as playmates.

title: 文福茶がま
author: 楠山 正雄
en: Long ago, in the country of Jōshū, at a temple called Morinji, there lived an old priest. The priest treasured a single iron tea-kettle. One day, he decided to use it to boil water for tea — but as the kettle grew hot on the fire, something very strange happened.

title: 瓜子姫子
author: 楠山 正雄
en: Long, long ago, in a certain place, there lived an old man and an old woman. They had no children, and felt terribly lonely. One day, as the old woman was washing clothes by the river, a large melon came floating down to her — bobbing on the current.

tokens.r: さかのうえのたむらまろ

title: 自然
en: A drop in the bucket. Speech is silver, silence is gold. A loaf of bread is better than the songs of birds. Flowers are yellow.

title: 自然 3
en: Wood burns. The tree fell down. I go to the mountain. The best swimmers are oftenest drowned.

title: 浦島太郎
author: 楠山 正雄
en: Long ago, in a certain place, there lived a fisherman named Urashima Tarō. One day, as he was on his way home from fishing, he came upon a group of children gathered on the beach, making a loud racket. They had caught a small turtle, and were striking it with sticks, throwing it about, and tormenting it in every way.

title: 和尚さんと小僧
titleEn: The Priest and the Little Monk
author: 楠山 正雄
en: Long ago, at a certain temple, there lived an old priest and one little novice monk. The priest was rather stingy, and whenever someone gave him sweets, he would hide them away so that he could eat them all by himself. The little monk, watching this, thought it most unfair.

title: 蜘蛛の糸
author: 芥川 龍之介
en: One day, the Buddha was wandering alone by the edge of the Pond of Lotuses in Paradise. The lotuses blooming on the pond were all perfect, white as jewels, and from their golden centres rose a fragrance that filled the air with an indescribable, exquisite scent.

title: 注文の多い料理店
author: 宮沢 賢治
en: Two young gentlemen, splendidly equipped like British soldiers, with shining new rifles slung over their shoulders, were tramping through the mountains at a place so deep that even the leaves seemed to whisper. With them they had brought two big dogs the colour of polar bears.

tokens.r: はちまんたろうよしいえ
ろくじょうほうがんためよし
ちんぜいはちろうためとも
